channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Rachat de SFR : faut-il craindre une hausse des prix des abonnements ?
item.description: Les forfaits mobiles ont déjà bondi de 31 % en un an en France. Si le démantèlement de SFR se confirme, la fin de la guerre des prix pourrait être actée.

item.title: Dyson’s handheld fan is more powerful and louder than I expected
item.description: Two years ago I attended a picturesque outdoor wedding in August where the hot and humid weather had guests occasionally ducking into their cars to enjoy a blast of AC. Dyson’s new $99.99 handheld fan would have provided some much-needed relief then, although I’m not sure I would have been comfortable powering it up during […]

item.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
item.description: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

item.title: DJI Avata 360 vs Antigravity A1: I compared the world's first 360 camera drones, and there was one clear winner
item.description: There are now two 360 drones available to buy, but is the DJI Avata 360 or the Antigravity A1 the better of these flying panoramic cameras?

item.title: France has ditched Windows 11 for Linux on 2.5 million government PCs — here's why Microsoft should worry that millions more could follow by the end of 2026
item.description: Is 2027 set to be the year of Linux? There are signs that this might actually be the case, for sure.

item.title: 'This device prevents sideloading': Amazon warns Fire TV Stick HD buyers that it can't be turned into an illegal 'Fire Stick' for free streaming
item.description: It's been confirmed that the new Amazon Fire TV Stick HD runs Vega OS and prevents sideloading.

item.title: On a testé la NIU KQi 100F, une trottinette électrique abordable dont les poignées se plient pour maximiser sa praticité
item.description: Plutôt stable, performante et confortable, la NIU KQi 100F se démarque surtout par sa praticité grâce aux poignées pliantes et son poids contenu, mais pèche par son freinage et son écran. Nous l'avons testée, voici notre verdict.

item.title: There’s nothing like an RPG over vacation
item.description: With a vacation comes a big choice: What game should I focus on during the trip? I thought about grinding out the harder levels of Super Meat Boy 3D, but I was looking for something more chill. I could have dabbled more with Slay the Spire II, but I already know that's a game I'll […]

item.title: The AI apps are coming for your PC
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 124, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, send me your Coachella fits, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) This week, I've been reading about restaurant bread and GLP-1s and Lenny Rachitsky and […]

item.title: I was one of the first to test Sony's latest Inzone headset, and it sets a new standard for open-back gaming audio
item.description: The Sony Inzone H6 Air sets a new standard for open-back gaming audio with its pristine sound and premium design.

item.title: New floating internet infrastructure takes flight
item.description: Sceye completes endurance testing for its high-altitude platform system, bringing floating internet infrastructure closer to rollout.

item.title: Concert de Céline Dion : pouvez-vous réclamer des dommages et intérêts si vous avez payé trop cher ?
item.description: Alors que le ministre du Commerce français a promis, mardi 14 avril, « des sanctions » après le fiasco des ventes de billets de la tournée de la chanteuse québécoise, les clients qui estiment avoir payé un prix abusif pourraient-ils réclamer un dédommagement ? Nous avons posé la question à Aude Guyon, avocate associée chez FBL avocats.
